# Problem Statement

The project addresses the problem of creating a community-based discussion system similar to platforms like Reddit or online forums.

In modern digital communication, users need platforms where they can:

- Share posts
- Discuss ideas
- Comment on topics
- Vote on content

This system provides a simplified model of real-world social platforms and demonstrates how object-oriented programming can be used to manage complex relationships efficiently.

# Project Description

This is a console-based discussion system in C++ using OOP principles.

Users can:

- Register and login
- Create communities
- Create posts
- Comment on posts
- Upvote/downvote posts

Data is stored in a file, and IDs are generated using a template-based ID generator.

ID generator. Posts and comments can be deleted, and user, community, post, and comment information is stored in a file.

# OOP Principles Identification

**Encapsulation**

Data is kept private and accessed using getters/setters.

**Inheritance**

Post and Comment inherit from Content class.

**Polymorphism**

display() function is overridden in Post and Comment.

**Abstraction**

Content is an abstract class with virtual function display().

**Composition**

- Community has Posts
- Post has Comments

**Association**

Post is linked to User.

**Templates**

IDGenerator class generates unique IDs for Users, Posts, and Comments.

# System Design & UML

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Figure 1 UML Diagram OF Community Discussion System

# Solution Definition

**Steps:**

- Load data from file
- User registers/login
- Create or view communities
- Create posts
- Add comments
- Upvote/downvote posts
- Save data on exit

# Design Pattern

Singleton Pattern is used in SystemManager to ensure only one object manages the whole system.

# Limitations & Future Work

**Limitations**

- File-based storage
- No password security
- Console-based only

**Future Work**

- Add database
- Add GUI
- Improve security
